new_classes.label_en: Other non-alcoholic beverages containing fat from dairy products of headings 0401–0404, of a fat content of less than 0.2% by weight (CN 2202 99 99)
new_classes.label_de: Andere nicht alkoholhaltige Getränke, mit einem Gehalt an Fetten aus Erzeugnissen der Positionen 0401–0404 von weniger als 0,2 GHT (KN 2202 99 99)
new_classes.definition_en: Non-alcoholic beverages, other than waters (heading 2201), flavoured/sweetened waters (CN 2202 10), and other non-alcoholic beverages not containing dairy fat or containing dairy fat at less than 0.2% by weight, falling under CN subheading 2202 99 99. Includes 'filled milk' (a product of skimmed milk or skimmed-milk powder to which refined vegetable fats or oils have been added) when immediately ready to drink, provided the dairy-fat content is less than 0.2% by weight.

coverage_explanation: The primary distinguishing criterion for CN 2202 99 99 versus its sibling 2202 99 95 is the fat content from dairy products (headings 0401–0404): this subheading covers products with a fat content of LESS THAN 0.2% by weight, while 2202 99 95 covers ≥ 0.2%. This threshold is captured via the decimalRange restriction on fatFromDairyByWeightPercent (maxExclusive 0.2) and the existing FilledMilkFatFromDairyQuality quality class, together with NonAlcoholicBeverageQuality to place the beverage in the correct non-alcoholic category. The legal note also mentions 'filled milk' as a specific product included here when ready-to-drink; this is captured in the class definition text but cannot be fully formalised in OWL without a dedicated FilledMilk class — a minor gap. The complement restriction against OtherNonAlcoholicBeverageDairyFat22029995 (the ≥ 0.2% sibling) is included for disjointness. A small coverage gap remains because the minimum fat threshold (fatFromDairyByWeightPercent ≥ 0, i.e., the product must actually contain some dairy fat) is implied but not explicitly lower-bounded, and the 'filled milk' description details (skimmed milk + vegetable fat) are not fully axiomatised in OWL.
